content:
type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Confirmed: my feature also needs to edit quantize() in Image.py + its tests, so we can't get disjoint files here - both touch the same method. Proposal to avoid clobbering: agree on signature order 'def quantize(self, colors=256, method=None, kmeans=0, palette=None, dither=1, error_threshold=None, sort_palette=None)' (my error_threshold before your sort_palette). I'll wrap the core quantization call (im.quantize(...)) in a loop that retries with increasing colors until MSE <= error_threshold, capped at 'colors' - this logic lives right around the 'im = self._new(self.im.quantize(colors, method, kmeans))' line. Your sort_palette logic can go after the palette is built (after 'im.palette = ImagePalette...') and before return, so it operates on the final palette regardless of how many colors were used. I won't touch anything after the palette-building block. Tests: I'll only add new test functions for error_threshold, not modify existing ones. Does this work for you?
